title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 4/30/2026 (Video)
Unilever Sales Boosted by Demand for Soap, Cleaning Products
summary: Unilever Plc’s sales rose more than expected as consumers in emerging markets including India snapped up its cleaning products and Dove soap, offsetting lackluster demand in the US.

title: Young: OPEC Exit is UAE Taking an Independent Stance
summary:
The UAE's decision to quit OPEC has been driven by its worsening rift with Saudi Arabia. Abu Dhabi is now also considering its membership of regional bodies, including the Arab League and the Organization of Islamic Cooperation, and is reassessing its role in the GCC. Karen Young, Senior Research Scholar in the Center on Global Energy Policy, Columbia University spoke to Bloomberg’s Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche on the oil market reaction to this move.

Original filename: 20260430_052530_UAE_s_Departure_from_OPEC_Raises_Geopolitical_Questions_Amid.mp4 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ECB Set to Hold Interest Rates as It Bides Time on Iran Response
summary: The European Central Bank is set to keep interest rates unchanged on Thursday as it continues to calibrate its response to the Iran war’s economic fallout.

title: Credit Agricole Misses on Profit, Capital Amid Volatility
summary: Credit Agricole SA’s first-quarter earnings missed analysts’ estimates while a key metric of capital strength declined, sending shares of the French lender lower.

title: French Economy Unexpectedly Failed to Grow as Iran Shock Hit
summary: France’s economy failed to grow while prices jumped more than expected, a sign that Europe may be succumbing to stagflationary threats emanating from the Iran war.

title: Copper Steadies After Five Days of Losses on Iran War Pessimism
summary: Copper steadied as traders assessed the possibility of an escalation in the US-Israeli war against Iran, and an expansion in Chinese manufacturing.

title: Yen Intervention Risk Builds as Central Banks Delay Rate Moves
summary: Investors increasingly see official intervention in the market as the only near-term lever to stem the yen’s slide, as central banks delay adjusting interest rates and Middle East tensions push oil prices higher.

title: Oil Hits Wartime High on Report US Eyeing Iran Military Options | Daybreak Europe 04/30/2026
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

On today's show, Brent crude rallied to a wartime high, spiking above $126 a barrel, after Axios reported that US President Donald Trump is being briefed on options for new military action in Iran.

Nasdaq 100 futures erased earlier gains that were driven by robust US tech earnings. Alphabet jumped after reporting a clear payoff for Google from its AI spending. But Meta tumbled after announcing another hike in capex. Traders now look ahead to Apple earnings.

Plus, the Federal Reserve held rates in a split vote. Four policymakers dissented.

Today's guests: Ida Lerner, ING, CFO & Stefan Dörfler, Erste Group, CFO. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Glencore’s Traders Score Big Profits as War Rattles Energy Markets
summary: Glencore Plc’s trading team made bumper profits as the Iran war roiled global commodity markets, with surging prices helping to put the firm’s marketing unit on course for one of its best-ever results.

title: Iran War Splits Asia’s Diesel Market Into Haves and Have-Nots
summary: The war in the Middle East has left Asian buyers grappling with a two-speed diesel economy, as poorer states face acute shortfalls, while wealthier nations with large refining industries enjoy sizable buffers.

title: BNP Paribas CFO: Took Provisions Against Macro Uncertainty
summary: The BNP Paribas CFO cited a ‘wait-and-see’ attitude from corporates in the first-quarter, and argued he took provisions against the macroeconomic uncertainties. That’s after France’s largest bank reported net income that beat estimates in the first quarter, driven by a strong performance in equities trading. The CFO Lars Machenil spoke exclusively to Bloomberg Television on the ‘Opening Trade’. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BP and Venezuela Sign Pact to Explore for Offshore Gas
summary: BP Plc and Venezuela agreed on a deal to explore for natural gas offshore as the South American country’s energy revival gathers pace following the US capture of Nicolas Maduro.

title: Asean Vows to Avoid Export Bans, Share Fuel as Oil Prices Soar
summary: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations vowed to avoid trade restrictions and push ahead with a fuel-sharing scheme as the region grapples with a spike in oil prices and supply disruptions due to the Iran war.

title: Europe’s Embattled Software Firms Rise Above AI, Iran Challenges
summary: Europe’s software companies are doing better than expected this earnings season, brushing off fears around artificial intelligence and the impact of the Iran war on business confidence.

title: Asia Growth at Risk as Oil Surges to Wartime High | Insight with Haslinda Amin 04/30/2026
summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Investindustrial Raises €1.5 Billion for Lower Mid-Market Fund
summary: Investindustrial has raised €1.5 billion ($1.75 billion) for its latest lower mid-market private equity fund, surpassing an initial target in a speedy four-month raise.

title: Higher Oil Prices May Spur Quicker Yield Gains in Emerging Asia
summary: Higher-for-longer oil prices threaten a faster rise in emerging Asian bond yields, if history is any guide — a risk market watchers say isn’t yet fully priced in.

title: Macron Moves to Name Top Ally Moulin to Lead Bank of France
summary: Emmanuel Macron’s chief of staff will leave next week, suggesting the French president is about to nominate a close ally to run the central bank.

title: CATL Share Sale Pressures HK Premium, Boosting Popular Trade
summary: A blockbuster share sale by Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. is poised to further chip away at the unusually high premium on the company’s Hong Kong-listed shares, potentially bringing them closer in line with its Shenzhen stock, analysts said.

title: ECB Set to Hold Rates as Policymakers Assess War Impact
summary: A unanimous survey of economists said the European Central Bank is set to keep interest rates unchanged at 2% on Thursday, as policymakers continue to weigh the economic impact of the Iran war. Bloomberg's Oliver Crook reports from Frankfurt. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BOE Expected to Keep Rates Unchanged at 3.75%
summary: The Bank of England is likely to keep interest rates on hold at 3.75% on Thursday, economists say, as policymakers balance the risk of a prolonged energy price shock from the Iran war against a subdued domestic economic picture. Bloomberg Economics' Matthew Bunny has a preview of the decision. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Naval Blockade Pushes Iran’s Currency to Record Low
summary: Iran’s currency has fallen in recent days to a fresh record low, in a sign the US’s naval blockade is squeezing the Islamic Republic’s economy.

title: FTSE 100 and Pound Hold Steady Ahead of BOE
China Manufacturing Expands Despite War, Wobbly Domestic Demand
summary: Chinese construction activity suffered its biggest slump since the early stages of the Covid pandemic and the services sector fell back into contraction, even as exports kept factories humming despite the disruptions caused by the war in Iran.

title: Demand at Japan’s Two-Year Note Sale Jumps to Highest Since 2024
summary: Japan’s two-year government bond auction on Thursday drew the strongest demand since August 2024, supported by higher yields and expectations that the Bank of Japan may not rush into further interest rate hikes.

title: India’s Rupee Drops to Record Low as Crude Surge Deepens Worries
summary: India’s rupee slid to a record low as crude prices continued their relentless surge, deepening worries about the nation’s external deficit.

title: Japan Utilities Withhold Earnings Guidance as Fuel Costs Bite
summary: Several Japanese power producers have withheld full-year earnings guidance due to uncertainty around the supply and cost of liquefied natural gas.
